relevant_snippets: AQR Capital Management 130K followers Cliff Asness and Dan Villalon’s latest piece dives even deeper into buffer funds—this time published in the Journal of Portfolio Management. While there’s more data and more co-authors (Antti Ilmanen and Jeffrey Cao join) than their previous two posts on the topic, the verdict hasn’t changed: buffered funds haven’t delivered on their promise. It’s not a short paper, so here’s the TL;DR: Buffer funds are complex, costly, and have underperformed in both theory and practice—and there are simpler, less expensive alternatives that are more effective when dealing with the risk of equity markets: https://bit.ly/3J8gmWJ

Research and Policy Center 12K followers A total portfolio approach (TPA) is gaining traction as investors rethink how portfolios are constructed, governed, and managed. But adopting TPA goes beyond asset allocation. It often requires rethinking team structures, incentive design, and investment committee decision-making. In a new two-part series from the Enterprising Investor Podcast, host Mona Naqvi speaks with Roger Urwin, to examine these considerations and the broader shift toward TPA. Part one explores strategic asset allocation as a precursor to TPA, the governance differences between them, and how they can co-exist. In part two, Urwin discusses why 2025 may represent a tipping point for TPA, the emergence of the chief total portfolio officer role, and the concept of TPA as a complete operating system. Listen to both conversations to examine how TPA is taking shape in practice. ▶ “Roger Urwin: From Strategic Asset Allocation to a Total Portfolio Mindset,” https://lnkd.in/eJBhknq6 ▶ “Roger Urwin: What a Total Portfolio Approach Looks Like in Practice,” https://lnkd.in/eYuUG2jk #InvestmentManagement #AssetAllocation

Cliff Asness and Dan Villalon’s latest piece dives even deeper into buffer funds—this time published in the Journal of Portfolio Management. While there’s more data and more co-authors (Antti Ilmanen and Jeffrey Cao join) than their previous two posts on the topic, the verdict hasn’t changed: buffered funds haven’t delivered on their promise. It’s not a short paper, so here’s the TL;DR: Buffer funds are complex, costly, and have underperformed in both theory and practice—and there are simpler, less expensive alternatives that are more effective when dealing with the risk of equity markets: https://bit.ly/3J8gmWJ
